game_info.title: Goose Chaos
game_info.concept:
It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose. Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/837470/Untitled_Goose_Game/“ --game_index 77713 --config configs/generators/threejs_gen.yaml --model google:gemini-3-pro-preview ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose. It's a lovely morning in the village and you are a horrible goose.

Untitled Goose Game is a slapstick-stealth-sandbox, where you are a goose let loose on an unsuspecting village. Make your way around town, from peoples' back gardens to the high street shops to the village green, setting up pranks, stealing hats, honking a lot, and generally ruining everyone’s day.

Featuring:

  • A horrible goose (that's you)
  • A town full of people just trying to get on with their day (you hate them)
  • A dedicated honk button (!!!)

AND ALSO

Oh dear! Two horrible geese!

You can now enjoy Untitled Goose Game with a friend, in a new two-player cooperative mode. Play through the whole game as two horrible geese, honking twice as much, teaming up to plan pranks, and generally ruining everyone's day, together.

  • A free update for all owners of Untitled Goose Game
  • Play through the entire game with two players
  • New goose, new honk, still horrible
game_info.description: You are a mischievous goose wreaking havoc in a peaceful village! Your goal is to complete pranks on unsuspecting villagers by stealing their items, honking to annoy them, and causing general chaos. Complete all the tasks on your to-do list to win the game. Avoid getting caught by villagers who will chase you if you get too close with their belongings. The village is your playground - explore gardens, shops, and the village green to find opportunities for mayhem!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move the goose in four directions
Space: HONK! (startle villagers and assert dominance)
Shift: Sprint (move faster, but makes more noise)
Z: Grab/Drop items (pick up objects to steal them or drop what you're carrying)
Enter: Start the game from the start screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart and return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic goose movement and navigation around the village. Verifying that the goose can move freely in all directions, avoid obstacles like fences and buildings, and navigate through different areas of the village without getting stuck.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the goose systematically through all areas of the map using arrow keys. Test movement in all four directions, verify collision detection with walls and obstacles, and ensure the goose can transition between different zones (garden, shops, village green).
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The goose should move smoothly in all directions, properly collide with obstacles without clipping through them, and successfully navigate the entire village map. The test is successful when the goose has visited all major areas without movement glitches.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing item interaction mechanics - grabbing, carrying, and dropping objects. Verifying that the goose can pick up stealable items, carry them while moving, and drop them at target locations for completing pranks.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate to items marked as stealable, press Z to grab them, move around while carrying the item, and drop it in different locations. Test multiple item types and verify that only one item can be carried at a time.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The goose should successfully pick up items with Z key, visually show the item being carried, move normally while carrying, and drop items on command. Items should appear at drop locations and the goose should be able to pick up different items sequentially.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing villager AI and chase mechanics. Verifying that villagers react appropriately to the goose's actions - ignoring it normally, becoming alert when items are stolen, and chasing the goose to retrieve their belongings.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Approach villagers without items to verify passive behavior. Then steal an item near a villager and observe if they chase. Test if villagers recover items when they catch the goose, and verify they return to normal behavior after recovery.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Villagers should patrol normally when the goose has no stolen items. When items are stolen, the corresponding villager should become alerted and chase the goose. If caught, the villager should recover their item and return to patrol. Test is successful when chase mechanics work consistently.

If caught, the villager should recover their item and return to patrol. Test is successful when chase mechanics work consistently.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing honking mechanics and villager reactions. Verifying that pressing space makes the goose honk, which startles nearby villagers and causes them to react with annoyance or fear.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Position the goose near villagers and press space to honk. Observe villager reactions at different distances. Test honking repeatedly and verify visual/animation feedback. Test if honking while carrying items has any special effects.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: The goose should produce a visible honk effect when space is pressed. Nearby villagers should show startled animations or move away from the goose. The honk should have a cooldown to prevent spam. Test succeeds when honk consistently affects villagers within range.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing sprint mechanics and stamina system. Verifying that holding shift makes the goose move faster, that there's a stamina limitation, and that sprinting has appropriate tradeoffs (more noise, attracts attention).
TEST_5.strategy_description: Hold shift while moving to test sprint speed. Monitor stamina depletion and regeneration. Test if sprinting near villagers increases detection range. Verify that stamina fully depletes and regenerates properly when not sprinting.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Sprint should significantly increase movement speed while shift is held. Stamina should visibly decrease during sprint and regenerate when walking. Sprinting should make the goose more noticeable to villagers. Test succeeds when sprint mechanics work smoothly with clear feedback.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing task completion and win condition. Verifying that the to-do list tracks completed pranks, updates correctly when tasks are finished, and triggers the win state when all tasks are completed.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Complete tasks systematically one by one by stealing items and delivering them to target locations. Monitor the to-do list UI for checkmarks. Verify each task completion is registered. Complete all tasks to trigger the win condition and game over screen.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each completed task should be marked on the to-do list with a visual indicator. The task count should update correctly. When all tasks are complete, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state and display victory message. Test succeeds when win condition triggers properly.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing comprehensive win strategy by completing all tasks efficiently. This is an end-to-end test that combines all mechanics to successfully complete the game from start to finish.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Start the game and systematically complete all tasks in an optimal order. Use sprint to move quickly between areas, honk to distract villagers when needed, grab items and deliver them to task locations, and avoid being caught. Use knowledge of villager patrol patterns to minimize detection.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: The AI should successfully complete all tasks on the to-do list within a reasonable time frame. Tasks should be completed efficiently using optimal pathing and timing. The game should end in a win state with all tasks checked off. Test succeeds when the AI consistently achieves victory.
